texto: I was afraid, sheer afraid, and wondered at myself. You see, I've no more pluck than any man of my inches but I'd been about a good bit. I'd seen adventure and heard other fellows talk it over, and I knew you're pretty sure to get out of everything with a whole skin till that last particular time that you don't - so what's the use of grizzling? ("Golden Baby")
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Alice Brown que provoca limites e escolhas conscientes. Ao questionar “I was afraid, sheer afraid, and wondered at myself. You see, I've no more pluck than any man of m…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre coragem, adventure, death, com foco em luto e coragem.

I'd seen adventure and heard other fellows talk it over, and I knew you're pretty sure to get out of everything with a whole skin till that last particular time that you don't - so what's the use of grizzling? texto: Something had lubricated us. Something had washed us clean. I understood, and at the same minute I understood that that they all understood, too. Hate had passed away, and in its place was the other word that's just as big. ("Golden Baby")
explicacao: Citação de Alice Brown sobre temas ligados a amor. “Something had lubricated us. Something had washed us clean. I understood, and at the same minute …” articula amor, hate, love em registro de amor, com relevância para contextos como love.

texto: You know some minutes warn you they're going to be mighty short and you'd better take a snapshot of 'em while you can. ("Golden Baby")
explicacao: Citação de Alice Brown sobre temas ligados a reflexao. “You know some minutes warn you they're going to be mighty short and you'd better take a snapshot …” articula reflexao, memory, minutes em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como reflexao.
